blocks.text: DTC P0097: Intake air temperature sensor 2 voltage low

Intake air temperature sensor 2 uses a thermistor to detect intake air temperature and compensate for changes in air density due to temperature. The electrical resistance of the intake air temperature sensor 2 varies with temperature. When the intake air temperature decreases, the electrical resistance increases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a higher voltage. When the intake air temperature increases, the electrical resistance decreases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a lower voltage. If the intake air temperature sensor 2 output voltage remains below a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch

blocks.text: Intake air temperature sensor 2 output voltage [intake air temperature sensor 2] remains below 0.08V for more than 2.0 seconds.

items.text: Ground fault in code (INTA line) between PGM-FI ECU and intake air temperature sensor 2
Failure of intake air temperature sensor 2

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
